_comment: Disk cleanup baseline - temp files, browser cache/cookies, Windows Update component-store cleanup, Recycle Bin. Nothing here runs blindly: Stage 1 audits what actually exists and has real size, Stage 2 builds the diff list, and Stage 3 only acts on items in that list (mandatory diff-list discipline, same as every other module pair).

tempFiles._description: Clears %TEMP%, C:\Windows\Temp, and every local user profile's AppData\Local\Temp folder.

paths.name: Per-User Temp
paths.description: Per-user temporary files (added for each user profile)

browsers._description: Applies to Chrome, Edge, and Firefox, for every local user profile found under C:\Users.
browsers._clearCookies_note: OFF by default: clearing cookies signs every user out of every website they're logged into. Set true only if that's actually wanted.

windowsUpdateCleanup._description: Runs DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup, but only when DISM's own analysis (/AnalyzeComponentStore) says cleanup is recommended - skips the expensive operation otherwise.
windowsUpdateCleanup._resetBase_note: DANGER: /ResetBase permanently removes the ability to uninstall currently-installed updates/service packs. Leave false unless you specifically want that irreversible extra space savings.

recycleBin._description: Empties the Recycle Bin on every fixed (non-removable) drive.
